diagnosis: The level is a massive horizontal corridor (100+ tiles wide) with almost no vertical traversal — the player spawns at the bottom, runs right across flat ground and occasional low platforms, and never gains meaningful elevation. Three falls cluster in the mid-to-late section (x≈2200–2700 px) where the terrain drops into deep, unclimbable pits; the player cannot recover and eventually gives up after 11+ minutes. The comment "needs some ladders" directly confirms the absence of upward mobility. Enemies (1, 2, 3) are present but placed on the same flat plane, so they don't compensate for the lack of verticality.

lessons.lesson: A level that spans hundreds of tiles horizontally but offers no way to climb or descend forces the player into a one-dimensional grind; even fair jumps and enemies cannot prevent disengagement when elevation never changes.
conditions: no ladders, climbable walls, or vertical traversal mechanics
player spawns at bottom with exit at similar elevation
lessons.outcome: player falls into unrecoverable pits deep in the level, spends 11+ minutes without completing, rates experience 2/5
